metadata.notes: Stroop-like interference (text-described), rule switching, n-back working memory, and inhibition. Stroop items state the printed word and ink color and ask the subject to name the ink color. Rule-switch items present sorted lists then ask for re-sorting under a new criterion. N-back items present a sequence and ask whether the current item matches one N back. Inhibition items name a forbidden response.

stimuli.prompt: The word 'BLUE' is printed in red ink. Name the ink color.
The word 'YELLOW' is printed in green ink. Name the ink color.
The word 'PURPLE' is printed in orange ink. Name the ink color.
The word 'BLACK' is printed in white on a grey background. Name the ink color.
The word 'TABLE' is printed in pink ink. Name the ink color.
The word 'RED' is printed in red ink. Name the ink color.
The number-word 'TWO' is printed three times: 'TWO TWO TWO'. How many words are there?
The number-word 'FIVE' appears twice: 'FIVE FIVE'. How many words are there?
The arrow symbol points LEFT but is labeled with the word 'RIGHT'. Which direction does the arrow point?
The word 'LOUD' is whispered. Was the sound loud or quiet?
Items: apple, hammer, banana, screwdriver, pear, wrench. First sort them by category (fruit vs tool). Now re-sort them by length of word (shortest to longest).
stimuli.expected: pear, apple, banana, hammer, wrench, screwdriver

stimuli.prompt: Items: 7, 22, 4, 19, 11, 3. First sort ascending. Now re-sort by whether each is odd or even, with odd numbers first.
Cards: red 4, blue 7, red 7, blue 4. First sort by color. Now switch — sort by number.
You have been alphabetizing names. The new rule is: sort by name length, breaking ties alphabetically. Sort: Tom, Sarah, Ben, Olivia, Mia.
Rule A: respond YES if the number is even. Rule B: respond YES if the number is greater than 10. We just switched from Rule A to Rule B. Number: 7. Answer?
Rule A: respond YES if the word starts with a vowel. Rule B: respond YES if the word has more than five letters. We are now using Rule B. Word: 'apple'. Answer?
You were grouping animals by habitat (land/sea/air). Now group these by number of legs (0, 2, 4): dolphin, eagle, snake, dog, parrot, octopus. Output: list each leg-count group.
stimuli.expected: 0 legs: dolphin, snake, octopus; 2 legs: eagle, parrot; 4 legs: dog

stimuli.prompt: Sequence: K, M, R, M. Is the current letter (M) the same as the one 2 back (R)?
Sequence: F, P, F. Is the current letter (F) the same as the one 2 back (F)?
Sequence: 4, 7, 2, 7. Is the current digit the same as the one 2 back?
Sequence: cat, dog, fish, dog. Is the current word the same as the one 2 back (fish)?
Sequence: B, A, T, M, A. Is the current letter (A) the same as the one 3 back (A)?
Sequence: 9, 9, 9, 9. Is the current digit the same as the one 1 back?
Sequence: red, blue, green, yellow, green. Is the current item the same as the one 2 back (green)?
Sequence: triangle, square, circle, triangle, square. Is the current item the same as the one 3 back (triangle)?
Repeat each word I say back to me, EXCEPT do not say the word 'banana'. Words: apple, banana, cherry, date.
Count from 1 to 10, but do NOT say the number 7. Output the sequence.

I will give you a list of animals. Say YES for each one EXCEPT mammals — say nothing for mammals. Animals: shark, dog, eagle, whale, lizard. Give your answers in order separated by commas (use '-' for skipped).
Read aloud the following words but skip any color word: house, blue, river, table, green, mountain.
When I say 'left', point right. When I say 'right', point left. I say: right.
List the days of the week in REVERSE order, but skip Wednesday.
stimuli.expected: Sunday, Saturday, Friday, Thursday, Tuesday, Monday

stimuli.prompt: When you see an even number, respond ODD. When you see an odd number, respond EVEN. Number: 8. Response?
Repeat the phrase 'pat the dog', but do NOT say the letter 'P' in any word. Reformulate.
stimuli.expected: any phrasing that omits the letter P, e.g., 'touch the dog'
